name: History-Resolved Phase with Adaptive Ruler
description: Core history-resolved phase update: the resolved phase theta_R is advanced by the wrapped difference between the raw principal phase theta_raw and the current theta_R, clipped to the adaptive phase ruler pi_a. This update underlies adaptive complex networks with S-gated conductance gain alpha_G, an adaptive pi_a bound, and controlled ablation modes.
source: RDM3DC / PR Root Guide (Phase-Lift + Adaptive-pi + ARP/AIN thread)
assumptions: wrap(x) returns a principal argument in (-pi, pi]
clip(x, a, b) saturates x to the interval [a,b]
pi_a > 0 and evolves according to an adaptive-ruler law
Full ablation modes allow turning off lift, adaptive ruler, and suppression
evidence: Simulation tests show that branch-resolved phase trajectories diverge from principal-branch baselines when winding histories differ despite identical present amplitudes
Parity-linked regime transitions emerge when adaptive pi_a and S-gated alpha_G are enabled
Ablation studies confirm that lifting and the adaptive ruler are necessary to observe branch-memory effects
